title: User-controlled web push endpoint can drive scheduled reminder SSRF

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: web_push_subscriptions stores endpoint/p256dh/auth/timezone without a database scheme, host, or push-service constraint

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: owner_all RLS lets any authenticated user manage their own subscription rows; direct API writes are not bound to browser PushManager

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: legitimate client upsert path shows the intended row shape but is only client-side validation

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: service-role cron worker passes stored endpoint to webPush.sendNotification

attacker_controlled_source: An authenticated user can call the Supabase table API for public.web_push_subscriptions and insert or update a row for their own user_id under the owner_all policy.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The database accepts arbitrary endpoint text and the scheduled service-role reminder worker later uses that endpoint in webPush.sendNotification without a server-side scheme/host allowlist or proof that it came from a real browser PushManager subscription.
impact: The scheduled Edge Function can be induced to make outbound requests to attacker-chosen URLs from the Supabase function environment. Depending on web-push endpoint validation and platform egress, this may support SSRF/probing or provider-cost abuse.
why_plausible: The only repository-enforced row control is user ownership. The browser API path is not a boundary because authenticated users can write the same table directly. web-push-reminders reads all rows with service_role and sends to sub.endpoint.
closest_apparent_control: RLS checks user_id but not endpoint shape or destination; VAPID encryption authenticates push payloads but does not prove the endpoint is a trusted push-service URL.

title: User-controlled web push endpoint can drive coach-push SSRF

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: shared web_push_subscriptions endpoint column has no destination constraint

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: owner_all RLS permits authenticated users to manage their own stored endpoints

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: coach-push reads all stored subscriptions with service_role and groups them by user

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: stored endpoint is passed to webPush.sendNotification for each user subscription

attacker_controlled_source: An authenticated user-controlled web_push_subscriptions row, reachable through Supabase table APIs under owner_all RLS.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The coach-push service-role cron worker trusts the stored endpoint as a push destination and sends to it without a server-side allowlist or subscription-origin proof.
impact: A separate scheduled service-role workflow can be made to issue outbound requests to attacker-selected endpoints when the coach_push flag and send-window conditions are met, creating an independently reachable SSRF/provider-abuse path.
why_plausible: coach-push has its own cron secret gate, but once cron runs it consumes user-writable endpoints. The missing endpoint validation is the same root control as CAND-W05-001, but this function is a distinct sink and scheduling path.
closest_apparent_control: Shared cron secret gates who can start the batch, not the destinations selected by stored user rows. The database has no scheme/host constraint.

title: Admin authorization trusts allowlisted email without repository-enforced verification state

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: requireAdmin authorizes solely from user.email membership in ADMIN_EMAILS

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: admin page access uses the same email-only allowlist

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: admin-only service-role user reset/verify/delete actions behind requireAdmin

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: admin-only feature flag and global notification mutations behind requireAdmin

attacker_controlled_source: A Supabase Auth user object and email claim. If project Auth settings allow unconfirmed email sign-in or an external provider returns an unverified/misbinding email, the repository code does not perform an additional verified-email check before granting admin.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The closest control is ADMIN_EMAILS membership, but the code does not check email_confirmed_at, app_metadata/provider email_verified state, an immutable admin role, or a database-backed admin membership table.
impact: Under permissive or misconfigured Auth email-verification settings, a non-admin could register/sign in using an allowlisted admin email and obtain service-role admin actions, including user deletion, email verification, password reset link generation, Pro grants, feature flags, and global notifications.
why_plausible: The repository has no Supabase Auth project configuration proving email confirmation is mandatory. Both page gating and server actions consume only user.email, so the same assumption controls all admin surfaces.
closest_apparent_control: ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist; absent repository-enforced verified-email/admin-role binding. Validation should inspect deployed Supabase Auth settings and provider claims.

title: Public OG card renderer lacks query length and numeric bounds before expensive ImageResponse rendering

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: unauthenticated query parameters are parsed without length/range limits for name/rank/streak/pct

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: badge name and tone feed rendered text/accent selection with no explicit input size cap

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Next ImageResponse renders a generated PNG for every request

vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The route constrains image dimensions but does not cap text input length or numeric ranges before Satori/Next OG image rendering.
impact: A public unauthenticated route may be usable for CPU/memory exhaustion or rendering-cost amplification with very long query values or high request volume. This is lower priority unless validation shows single-request resource exhaustion or missing platform rate limits.
why_plausible: The route is intentionally public for social sharing and directly uses searchParams to construct text rendered into an image. There is no repository-local rate limit, auth, or maximum length check.
closest_apparent_control: Fixed width/height and fallback tone constrain output shape, but not input size or renderer work.

title: Authenticated support-email function bypasses client/database feedback bounds before sending email

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: function authenticates any Supabase user JWT

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: message is only checked for nonempty; rating/category are weakly normalized and no max length/rate limit is enforced

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: attacker-controlled message is sent through Resend to the support inbox

affected_locations.label: closest_control
affected_locations.detail: client/database feedback path enforces validation, but direct function invocation bypasses it

attacker_controlled_source: Any authenticated user can invoke the support-email Edge Function directly with arbitrary JSON body.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The function does not reuse validateFeedback or enforce the database 10..2000 character message and 1..5 rating constraints before sending via Resend.
impact: Authenticated users can send oversized or repeated support emails, increasing provider cost and support-inbox abuse. HTML injection is mitigated by escapeHtml, so this remains a lower-priority abuse candidate rather than a content-injection finding.
why_plausible: The function is callable directly through Supabase Functions with a user JWT; the database insert path is separate and not required before the Resend send.
closest_apparent_control: JWT authentication and HTML escaping exist, but no server-side size, rating, or rate control on the email function itself.
